chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. Criminal Bail Application No.S-978 of 2025 Applicant: Rashid @ Rashi s/o Muhammad Uris through Mr. Shahzad Ahmed Mallah, Advocate. Complainant: Syed Arslan Badshah s/o Syed Mir Badshah present in Person. State: The State through Mr. Khalid Hussain Lakho, D.P.G. alongwith ASI Ghulam Abbas Leghari PS City Tando Muhammad Khan. Date of hearing: 28.10.2025 Date of Order: 28.10.2025 O R D E R JAWAD AKBAR SARWANA J:- Through instant Bail Application, applicant named above seeks post arrest bail in Crime No.24 of 2025 registered under Sections 397, 394, 34 PPC at Police Station Tando Muhammad Khan City, after his bail plea was declined by the learned Sessions Judge, Tando Muhammad Khan vide order dated 08.08.2025. 2. The details and particulars of the F.I.R. are already available in the bail application and F.I.R., same could be gathered from the copy of F.I.R. attached with such application, hence needs not to reproduce the same hereunder. 3. At the outset, complainant Syed Arslan Badshah (CNIC No.41308- 9321746-5) is present and states that due to intervention of their Nek-Mards of locality, they have compromised with the applicant Rashid @ Rashi and has filed No Objection affidavit which is taken on record. 4. On the other hand, in view of the no objection rendered by the complainant, learned D.P.G has also raised his no objection for grant of bail to the applicant and states that the offence with which the applicant stands charged is compoundable. 5. In view of the above, since the complainant rendered his No Objection as above, the applicant is granted post arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) and P.R Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

On the other hand, in view of the no objection rendered by the complainant, learned D.P.G has also raised his no objection for grant of bail to the applicant and states that the offence with which the applicant stands charged is compoundable. 5. In view of the above, since the complainant rendered his No Objection as above, the applicant is granted post arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) and P.R Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. JUDGE
